model_narrative: We plan a year of mixed cropping on a small family farm. We decide how many hectares to plant of each crop, and month by month how much of the family's labor to work on the farm, how much to hire out for wages, and how much extra temporary labor to bring in. Selling crops brings in revenue and hiring family labor out brings in wages, while temporary labor and the cash costs of growing reduce what we keep. The objective is to maximize the net farm income over the whole year.

sets.name: c
sets.doc: the crops the farm can grow

sets.name: t
sets.doc: the months of the planning year, given in calendar order

params.name: landreq
params.doc: the share of a hectare that one hectare of a crop occupies in a given month, in hectares of land tied up per hectare grown; a value of zero means the crop occupies no land that month

params.name: laborreq
params.doc: the labor needed to tend one hectare of a crop in a given month, in man-days per hectare; a value of zero means the crop needs no labor that month

params.name: yield_param
params.doc: the harvested output of a crop, in tons per hectare grown; stored on the attribute model.yield_param because yield is a reserved word

params.name: price
params.doc: the selling price of a crop, in dollars per ton of output

params.name: miscost
params.doc: the miscellaneous cash cost of growing a crop, in dollars per hectare grown

params.name: land
params.doc: the total cultivable area of the farm, in hectares

params.name: famlab
params.doc: the family labor available each month, in man-days; the same amount is available every month

params.name: owage
params.doc: the wage the family earns when it hires its labor out, in dollars per man-day

params.name: twage
params.doc: the wage paid for temporary hired-in labor, in dollars per man-day

params.name: dpm
params.doc: the number of working days in a month

vars.name: xcrop
vars.doc: the area planted to each crop, in hectares

vars.name: yfarm
vars.doc: the net farm income over the year, in dollars

vars.name: revenue
vars.doc: the gross value of crop production over the year, in dollars

vars.name: mcost
vars.doc: the total miscellaneous cash cost over the year, in dollars

vars.name: labcost
vars.doc: the total cost of temporary hired-in labor over the year, in dollars

vars.name: labearn
vars.doc: the total income earned by hiring family labor out over the year, in dollars

vars.name: flab
vars.doc: the family labor used on the farm in each month, in man-days

vars.name: fout
vars.doc: the family labor hired out off the farm in each month, in man-days

vars.name: tlab
vars.doc: the temporary labor hired in for the farm in each month, in man-days

description: The farm has a fixed area of cultivable land, and crops planted in the same month all draw on that same land at once. For each month, the land tied up by the crops growing that month, summed across every crop according to how much area each one occupies, must not exceed the total area of the farm.

description: Tending the crops takes labor, and that labor has to come from somewhere. For each month, the labor required by the crops growing that month, summed across every crop, must be covered by the family labor worked on the farm plus any temporary labor hired in that month.

description: The family has a fixed amount of labor available each month, and every bit of it is either worked on the farm or hired out for wages. For each month, the family labor available must equal the family labor used on the farm plus the family labor hired out that month.

description: Crop production has a gross value that comes from selling what is harvested. Set the gross value of production equal to the sum across every crop of the area planted to it times its yield per hectare times its selling price.
expected_pyomo: model.arev = Constraint(expr=model.revenue == sum(model.yield_param[c] * model.price[c] * model.xcrop[c] for c in model.c))

description: Growing crops incurs miscellaneous cash costs that scale with how much is planted. Set the total miscellaneous cash cost equal to the sum across every crop of the area planted to it times its cash cost per hectare.
expected_pyomo: model.acost = Constraint(expr=model.mcost == sum(model.miscost[c] * model.xcrop[c] for c in model.c))

description: Net farm income is what is left after the year's activity is tallied up. Set the net farm income equal to the gross value of production plus the income from hiring labor out, minus the cost of temporary labor and minus the miscellaneous cash costs of growing.
expected_pyomo: model.income = Constraint(expr=model.yfarm == model.revenue + model.labearn - model.labcost - model.mcost)

description: Generate the complete constraint set for this model.
expected_pyomo:
def landbal_rule(model, t):
    return sum(model.landreq[t, c] * model.xcrop[c] for c in model.c) <= model.land
model.landbal = Constraint(model.t, rule=landbal_rule)
def laborbal_rule(model, t):
    return sum(model.laborreq[t, c] * model.xcrop[c] for c in model.c) <= model.flab[t] + model.tlab[t]
model.laborbal = Constraint(model.t, rule=laborbal_rule)
def flabor_rule(model, t):
    return model.famlab == model.flab[t] + model.fout[t]
model.flabor = Constraint(model.t, rule=flabor_rule)
model.arev = Constraint(expr=model.revenue == sum(model.yield_param[c] * model.price[c] * model.xcrop[c] for c in model.c))
model.acost = Constraint(expr=model.mcost == sum(model.miscost[c] * model.xcrop[c] for c in model.c))
model.alab = Constraint(expr=model.labcost == sum(model.twage * model.tlab[t] for t in model.t))
model.aout = Constraint(expr=model.labearn == sum(model.owage * model.fout[t] for t in model.t))
model.income = Constraint(expr=model.yfarm == model.revenue + model.labearn - model.labcost - model.mcost)
